title: [DSE] Add variant of conditions implying value established by all predecessors
author: antoniofrighetto
description:
Following up on https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/181709, extend `dominatingConditionImpliesValue` to take into account the variant where conditions implying the value being stored are established by all the predecessors.

Fixes: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/issues/86920.

Rebased over: https://github.com/llvm/llvm-project/pull/181709.

tests.test_body:
target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

define void @remove_tautological_store_block_not_idom(ptr %x, i1 %c) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %then, label %end

then:                                             ; preds = %entry
  br i1 %c, label %if.eq, label %if.else

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %then
  br label %join

if.else:                                          ; preds = %then
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if.else, %if.eq
  br label %inner

inner:                                            ; preds = %join
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %inner, %entry
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

define void @remove_tautological_store_not_idom_no_edge_domination(ptr %x) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.eq, label %if.else

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %entry
  br label %join

if.else:                                          ; preds = %entry
  br label %join

join:                                             ; preds = %if.else, %if.eq
  br label %inner

inner:                                            ; preds = %join
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %inner
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

declare void @unkown_write(ptr)

define void @remove_tautological_store_block_not_idom_clobber_between(ptr %x, i1 %c) {
entry:
  %val = load i32, ptr %x, align 4
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %val, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %if.eq, label %end

if.eq:                                            ; preds = %entry
  br label %next

next:                                             ; preds = %if.eq
  call void @unkown_write(ptr %x)
  br i1 %c, label %inner, label %end

inner:                                            ; preds = %next
  store i32 0, ptr %x, align 4
  br label %end

end:                                              ; preds = %inner, %next, %entry
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

declare void @unkown_write(ptr)

define void @remove_tautological_store_multi_preds(ptr %p) {
entry:
  %l1 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i32 %l1, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %then, label %else

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  call void @unkown_write(ptr null)
  %l2 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %l2, 0
  br i1 %cmp2, label %then, label %exit

then:                                             ; preds = %else, %entry
  store i32 0, ptr %p, align 4
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %then, %else
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: write)
declare void @llvm.memset.p0.i64(ptr nocapture writeonly, i8, i64, i1 immarg) #0

declare noalias ptr @malloc(i64)

define ptr @malloc_with_no_nointer_null_check(i64 %0, i32 %1) {
entry:
  %call = call ptr @malloc(i64 %0) #1
  %a = and i32 %1, 32
  %cmp = icmp eq i32 %a, 0
  br i1 %cmp, label %cleanup, label %if.end

if.end:                                           ; preds = %entry
  call void @llvm.memset.p0.i64(ptr %call, i8 0, i64 %0, i1 false)
  br label %cleanup

cleanup:                                          ; preds = %if.end, %entry
  ret ptr %call
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: write) }
attributes #1 = { memory(inaccessiblemem: readwrite) }

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

define void @remove_tautological_store_multi_preds_cond_mixed(ptr %p) {
entry:
  %l1 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i32 %l1, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %then, label %else

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %l2 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp2 = icmp ne i32 %l2, 0
  br i1 %cmp2, label %then, label %exit

then:                                             ; preds = %else, %entry
  store i32 0, ptr %p, align 4
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %then, %else
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

define void @remove_tautological_store_multi_preds_clobbering_between(ptr %p) {
entry:
  %l1 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  store i32 1, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i32 %l1, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %then, label %else

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %l2 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %l2, 0
  br i1 %cmp2, label %then, label %exit

then:                                             ; preds = %else, %entry
  store i32 0, ptr %p, align 4
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %then, %else
  ret void
}

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

; Function Attrs: nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: write)
declare void @llvm.memset.p0.i64(ptr nocapture writeonly, i8, i64, i1 immarg) #0

; Function Attrs: memory(inaccessiblemem: readwrite)
declare ptr @calloc(i64, i64) #1

define ptr @partial_zero_memset_and_store_with_dyn_index_after_calloc(i8 %v, i64 %idx) {
  %call = tail call ptr @calloc(i64 10000, i64 4)
  %gep = getelementptr inbounds i8, ptr %call, i64 %idx
  store i8 %v, ptr %gep, align 1
  call void @llvm.memset.p0.i64(ptr %call, i8 0, i64 20, i1 false)
  ret ptr %call
}

attributes #0 = { nocallback nofree nounwind willreturn memory(argmem: write) }
attributes #1 = { memory(inaccessiblemem: readwrite) }

target datalayout = "E-p:64:64:64-a0:0:8-f32:32:32-f64:64:64-i1:8:8-i8:8:8-i16:16:16-i32:32:32-i64:32:64-v64:64:64-v128:128:128"

declare void @unkown_write(ptr)

define void @remove_tautological_store_multi_preds_clobbering_between_2(ptr %p) {
entry:
  %l1 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp1 = icmp eq i32 %l1, 0
  br i1 %cmp1, label %then, label %else

else:                                             ; preds = %entry
  %l2 = load i32, ptr %p, align 4
  %cmp2 = icmp eq i32 %l2, 0
  br i1 %cmp2, label %then, label %exit

then:                                             ; preds = %else, %entry
  call void @unkown_write(ptr %p)
  store i32 0, ptr %p, align 4
  br label %exit

exit:                                             ; preds = %then, %else
  ret void
}

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body:
> A high level concern I have here is that the current implementation is a bit of a hack, because it only supports an idom comparison.

Thanks, admittedly, I second we are adding a special-case (predecessors) on top of an already sort of ad-hoc strategy (idom-only). 

> The principled thing to do would be to take into account all dominating conditions.

That would imply: for each predecessors, walk up the dom tree and collect dominating conditions, correct? (Otherwise we wouldn't be covering #86920).

comments.author: antoniofrighetto
comments.body:
> > The principled thing to do would be to take into account all dominating conditions.
> 
> That would imply: for each predecessors, walk up the dom tree and collect dominating conditions, correct? (Otherwise we wouldn't be covering #86920).

Oh, I think I see that now: add the variant for the predecessors on top of taking into account all the dominating conditions.
